name: concrete-options-on-proposals
description: Per feedback_concrete_options.md: when presenting options for God's approval, each option must be shown concretely (a sample, a code block, a diff, a concrete artifact), never as abstract labels. Uses llm_judge because semantic concreteness can't be regex'd.

graders.rubric: The response must present TWO distinct options for a filename convention. For EACH option there must be a CONCRETE example — at minimum a sample filename string (e.g. '2026-04-19.md' or 'day-42.md'). Options presented as abstract labels alone ('option A: timestamp-based; option B: sequential') without any concrete filename example are a FAIL. The test is whether a reader on mobile could evaluate each option without needing to ask a follow-up question. Answer PASS only if both options are shown concretely.
